quest.guide.drawers.compacters.desc_1: &bCompacting Drawers&r allow items that have &6compressed&r forms &o&7(for example, Nuggets->Ingots->Blocks)&r to exist in both their compressed and uncompressed forms simultaneously.
quest.guide.drawers.compacters.desc_2: The maximum amount they can hold is decided by the &6most compressed form&r of the item.
quest.guide.drawers.compacters.subtitle: Existence in all forms
quest.guide.drawers.compacters.title: Compacting Drawers
quest.guide.drawers.controller.desc_1: &bDrawer Controllers&r can read the contents of any drawers that are connected to it within a &650&r block radius.
quest.guide.drawers.controller.desc_2: This can be done by placing drawers directly adjacent to it, using drawer trims, or by using the &bRemote Upgrade&r.
quest.guide.drawers.controller.desc_3: &6[Right-Clicking]&r on it with any item already in the drawer network will automatically try to store the item in the respective drawer.
quest.guide.drawers.controller.desc_4: While players can only &cdeposit&r items through the Drawer Controller, other mods such as Refined Storage's &bExternal Storage&r and Pipez's &bItem Pipe&r can request items from the drawer network.
quest.guide.drawers.controller.subtitle: All in one place
quest.guide.drawers.controller.title: Drawer Controller
quest.guide.drawers.frame.desc_1: The &bFraming Table&r allows applying block textures to drawers.
quest.guide.drawers.frame.subtitle: Fancy Drawers
quest.guide.drawers.frame.title: Framing Table
quest.guide.drawers.hidekey.subtitle: Now you see me...
quest.guide.drawers.hidekey.title: Concealment Key
quest.guide.drawers.htierthreecd.desc_1: Holds items with up to &bthree&r levels of compression.
quest.guide.drawers.htierthreecd.desc_2: &o&7(for example, Iron Nuggets, Iron Ingots, and Iron Blocks)&r
quest.guide.drawers.htierthreecd.desc_3: Holds up to &664&r stacks of the most compact form, before upgrades.
quest.guide.drawers.htierthreecd.title: &eTier 3&r Compacting Half Drawer
quest.guide.drawers.htiertwocd.desc_1: Holds items with up to &btwo&r levels of compression.
quest.guide.drawers.htiertwocd.desc_2: &o&7(for example, Iron Ingots and Iron Blocks)&r
quest.guide.drawers.htiertwocd.desc_3: Holds up to &664&r stacks of the most compact form, before upgrades.
quest.guide.drawers.htiertwocd.title: &eTier 2&r Compacting Half Drawer
quest.guide.drawers.keyring.desc_1: Allows multiple keys to be stored as a single item.
quest.guide.drawers.keyring.desc_2: Add keys by picking them up and &6[Right-Clicking]&r them on the key ring.
quest.guide.drawers.keyring.desc_3: Remove keys by &6[Right-Clicking]&r the key ring.
quest.guide.drawers.keyring.subtitle: The One Ring
quest.guide.drawers.keyring.title: Key Ring
quest.guide.drawers.lockerkey.desc_1: The &bDrawer Key&r is used to lock a drawer to only accept the type of item it already contains. If it is empty, it will lock to the first item placed into it.
quest.guide.drawers.lockerkey.desc_2: &6[Right-Click]&r a drawer to apply the key's affect. Right clicking a &bDrawer Controller&r will apply the key's effect to all drawers in the network.
quest.guide.drawers.lockerkey.subtitle: Special Abilities
quest.guide.drawers.lockerkey.title: Drawer Key
quest.guide.drawers.prioritykey.desc_1: Allows assigning a &bpriority level&r to each drawer, making them recive the inputs from a &bDrawer Controller&r according to their priority level.
quest.guide.drawers.prioritykey.desc_2: &6[Right-Click]&r on a drawer to cycle between priority levels.
quest.guide.drawers.prioritykey.subtitle: Mother's favourite child
quest.guide.drawers.prioritykey.title: Priority Key
quest.guide.drawers.puller.desc_1: The &bDrawer Puller&r allows removing drawer slots from a drawer while keeping their contents.
quest.guide.drawers.puller.desc_2: This allows you to move items around without having to break and replace the drawer itself.
quest.guide.drawers.puller.desc_3: &cNOTE:&r Contents of a drawer slot cannot be accessed until placed back into a drawer!
quest.guide.drawers.puller.subtitle: I always wanted to be a dentist
quest.guide.drawers.puller.title: Drawer Puller
quest.guide.drawers.quantifykey.desc_1: Makes drawers display the quantity of contained items.
quest.guide.drawers.quantifykey.subtitle: Show me the goods
quest.guide.drawers.quantifykey.title: Quantify Key
quest.guide.drawers.slave.desc_1: The &bDrawer Controller Slave&r acts as an extension for Drawer Controller, allowing another point of access to the drawer network.
quest.guide.drawers.slave.desc_2: While there can only be &cone&r Drawer Controller per network, there can be multiple Drawer Controller Slaves.
quest.guide.drawers.slave.subtitle: Master got me working
quest.guide.drawers.slave.title: Drawer Controller Slave
quest.guide.drawers.storage.desc_1: &bStorage Upgrades&r increase the stack capacity of drawers.
quest.guide.drawers.storage.desc_2: The total amount of stacks the drawer can store is its &6base stack value&r multiplied by the &bsum&r of all &6stack upgrades&r.
quest.guide.drawers.storage.desc_3: &7&oExample: A 1x1 drawer has a base capacity of 32 stacks.&r
quest.guide.drawers.storage.desc_4: &7&oAdding a Stack Upgrade (V) and a Stack Upgrade (II) would multiply the total by 64 + 4 = 68x&r
quest.guide.drawers.storage.desc_5: &7&oThis gives a final capacity of 32 x 68 = 2176 stacks!&r
quest.guide.drawers.storage.subtitle: Stacks and stacks and stacks...
quest.guide.drawers.storage.title: Storage Upgrades
quest.guide.drawers.tierthreecd.desc_1: Holds items with up to &bthree&r levels of compression.
quest.guide.drawers.tierthreecd.desc_2: &o&7(for example, Iron Nuggets, Iron Ingots, and Iron Blocks)&r
quest.guide.drawers.tierthreecd.desc_3: Holds up to &6128&r stacks of the most compact form, before upgrades.
quest.guide.drawers.tierthreecd.title: &eTier 3&r Compacting Drawer
quest.guide.drawers.tiertwocd.desc_1: Holds items with up to &btwo&r levels of compression.
quest.guide.drawers.tiertwocd.desc_2: &o&7(for example, Iron Ingots and Iron Blocks)&r
quest.guide.drawers.tiertwocd.desc_3: Holds up to &6128&r stacks of the most compact form, before upgrades.
quest.guide.drawers.tiertwocd.title: &eTier 2&r Compacting Drawer
quest.guide.drawers.trims.desc_1: &bDrawer Trims&r allows connecting drawers to a drawer network without needing to use normal drawers to bridge the gap.
quest.guide.drawers.trims.subtitle: Fancy a game of snake?
quest.guide.drawers.trims.title: Drawer Trims
quest.guide.drawers.upgrades.desc_1: &bDrawer Upgrades&r can be added to a drawer to increase its storage size or grant it additional abilities.
quest.guide.drawers.upgrades.desc_2: Players can view a drawer's upgrades by opening the interface of the drawer using &6[SHIFT + RMB]&r.
quest.guide.drawers.upgrades.subtitle: Upgrades people, upgrades!
quest.guide.drawers.upgrades.title: Drawer Upgrades
quest.guide.hordebuilding.desc_1: Buildings marked as &bHorde Buildings&r in the guidebook's &6Districts&r chapter§r will always contain a §chorde§r of infected.
quest.guide.hordebuilding.desc_2: The horde &cmust be defeated&r before you can loot any chests in the building.
quest.guide.hordebuilding.desc_3: Kill all &bglowing infected&r until you get a message that the horde has been cleared.
quest.guide.hordebuilding.desc_4: &cWARNING:&r As no infected will spawn, &chorde buildings cannot be cleared on the first day&r! You should use the time to properly prepare, or use a bed to sleep through the first night.
quest.guide.hordebuilding.subtitle: DONT DEAD OPEN INSIDE
quest.guide.hordebuilding.title: Horde Buildings
quest.guide.hordewave.desc_1: Infected hordes show up every &6&l15&r&r days server time. This counts &bactual in-game days passed&r — so sleeping &cWILL&r speed up the timer!
quest.guide.hordewave.desc_2: The difficulty of the horde depends on &6individual player playtime&r.
quest.guide.hordewave.desc_3: Each subsequent horde will be §cstronger§r than the last, so be sure to upgrade your gear and prepare defenses for your base!
quest.guide.hordewave.desc_4: Looting or crafting a §bhorde calendar§r and right clicking it will show you how many days are left until the next horde.
quest.guide.hordewave.subtitle: 15 days to Die
quest.guide.hordewave.title: Horde Waves
quest.guide.hotkeys.subtitle: Beginners Guide to Modded Minecraft
quest.guide.hotkeys.title: Useful Hotkeys
quest.guide.items.03839ABAB35BCDAE.desc_: Allows players to check their &eDPS&r with weapons.
&cNote:&r The Target Dummy can also be equipped with armor like an armour stand!
&o&7To measure single shot damage, also consider crafting the &bHumanoid Target&7 from TacZ!&r
quest.guide.items.0B4E62DDF624C0FE.desc_: The Maintenance Kit acts like a &bportable Engineer's Workbench&r, allowing the player to change the upgrades on their tools on the go.
quest.guide.items.18354E44F2D2BD50.desc_: Creates a &btemporary block&r in the air, which allows the player to replace it with any other block in their hand.
quest.guide.items.18354E44F2D2BD50.subti: Not to be confused with the Trinket
quest.guide.items.239DBC9B44A01B99.desc_: Allows easy measuring of &bdistances&r between blocks.
quest.guide.items.2F1F6BA0891AD306.desc_: The &bHypertube Entrance Block&r allows a player to enter the hypertube network. It needs to be provided with &eRPM&r to function.
The higher the &eRPM&r, the faster the travel time.
Two Hypertube Entrances can be placed on either end of a hypertube network to allow for two-way trael.
Enter the hypertube by pressing &6[Shift]&r when near it, or set it to automatically suck in players by &6[Right-Clicking]&r on it with a &bWrench.&r
&o&7Ponder this block by mousing over it and holding &6[W] &7for a detailed guide.&r
quest.guide.items.3AEA598A9736C383.desc_: Each category can be configured independently.
quest.guide.items.409C5F7F5737A7E2.desc_: The &bHypertube Accelerator Block&r is used for accelerating or decelerating a player inside a hypertube network. It needs to be provided with &eRPM&r to function.
The higher the &eRPM&r, the greater the effect.
By default, players travelling through it will be &bsped up&r. Changing the accelerator to Brake mode by &6[Right-Clicking]&r it with a wrench will cause it to &cslow down&r players instead.
&o&7Ponder this block by mousing over it and holding &6[W] &7for a detailed guide.&r
quest.guide.items.40F832D775FD3ECE.desc_: The &bFilter Destination Tool&r allows players to control where the &boutput&r of a pipe will be sent.
While holding the tool, &6[Right-Click]&r the target container where you want the output to be sent.
&cNOTE:&r The face you click on must be the same face as where the pipe is connected to!
Once the target container has been set, &6[Right-Click]&r a pipe in extraction mode, click on the &e\
&r button, then place the configured &bFilter Destination Tool&r in the provided slot.
Lastly, provide an &bitem&r or &btag&r to filter in the Item slot, then click &e\
&r. If everything is set up correctly, that item will now be sent to the target container.
quest.guide.items.415112593E280292.desc_: Can be configured to drain a fixed amount of energy.
quest.guide.items.4355BE33A62714EB.desc_: Enables control of extraction using redstone.
quest.guide.items.4910B2BFC70EDD9E.desc_: Enables pipes to control where items are sent.
quest.guide.items.49E2031F51FCA179.desc_: Can be configured to whitelist or blacklist certain items.
quest.guide.items.4ED029DD9DAF8C0C.desc_: Places a &brandom block&r from the player's inventory.
quest.guide.items.52194775B3C7F1F7.desc_: &cDestroys&r liquids placed or pumped into it.
Can be configured to whitelist or blacklist certain liquids.
quest.guide.items.74FC4FD1AA60CBDA.desc_: Without access to the End, the &bCollapsible Glider&r is an alternative to the Elytra.
However, it &ccannot be repaired&r, and must be replaced.
quest.guide.items.7BDB00E0CDF211C7.desc_: &bHypertube Blocks&r are used to extend the hypertube network.
&6[Right-Click]&r on a Hypertube Entrance, Hypertube Accelerator or another Hypertube Block to enter placement mode, then &6[Right-Click]&r again to confirm placement.
&o&7Ponder this block by mousing over it and holding &6[W] &7for a detailed guide.&r
quest.guide.items.7C88420A853202C4.desc_: Combines the function of all three types of pipe.
&cWARNING:&r This item is known to cause lag and FPS drops. Use sparingly!
quest.guide.items.accbackpack.desc_1: The &bAccumulator Backpack&r is a wearable that can hold accumulators inside of it, allowing players to carry around a supply of FE.
quest.guide.items.accbackpack.desc_2: This is particularly useful when using the &bRailgun&r for long durations due to its small energy capacity.
quest.guide.items.accbackpack.desc_3: &7&oCharged &bAccumulators &7can be placed inside the Accumulator Backpack using the &bEngineer's Workbench&7, or the entire backpack can be charged using a &bCharging Station&r.
quest.guide.items.accbackpack.subtitle: Adding charge
quest.guide.items.accbackpack.title: Accumulator Backpack
quest.guide.items.acid.desc_1: Equivalent to giving the Mining Drill Fortune III.
quest.guide.items.acid.title: Rock Softening Acid
quest.guide.items.airtankl.desc_1: Allows the drill to operate underwater.
quest.guide.items.airtankl.title: Pressurized Airtank
quest.guide.items.ajetpack.title: Andesite Jetpack
quest.guide.items.augers.desc_1: Increases the speed of the mining drill. Stacks up to 3.
quest.guide.items.augers.subtitle: Basically Mandatory
quest.guide.items.augers.title: Additional Augers
quest.guide.items.backtank.desc_1: Backtanks allow players to store &bPressurized Air&r for tools that require it to function.
quest.guide.items.backtank.desc_2: You can refill it by providing &eRotational Force&r to the top of the tank, when it is placed on the ground.
quest.guide.items.backtank.desc_3: The higher the RPM, the faster the refilling speed.
quest.guide.items.backtank.desc_4: &o&7You can view extra information about this item by hovering over it and pressing &6[Shift]&7.&r
quest.guide.items.backtank.title: Copper Backtank
quest.guide.items.bjetpack.title: Brass Jetpack
quest.guide.items.bladequiver.desc_1: Allows you to carry two additional sawblades.
quest.guide.items.bladequiver.title: Blade Quiver
quest.guide.items.burner.desc_3: &o&7You can view extra information about this item by hovering over it and pressing &6[Shift]&7.&r
quest.guide.items.burner.subtitle: Falling, with Style
quest.guide.items.burner.title: &ePortable Drill
quest.guide.items.buzzsaw.desc_1: With tree falling enabled for all axes, the buzzsaw is obsolete for its intended purpose.
quest.guide.items.buzzsaw.desc_2: However, equipping it with a &bRockcutter Blade&r gives it the &bSilk Touch&r enchantment, which may be useful for players struggling to find the necessary upgrade part.
quest.guide.items.buzzsaw.desc_3: &cNote:&r The Buzzsaw requires Sawblades and Fuel &e(Gasoline, Diesel, Biodiesel)&r to function.
quest.guide.items.buzzsaw.desc_4: &7&oPlace the Buzzsaw on the &bEngineer's Workbench &7to add sawblades and modify its attachments.&r
quest.guide.items.buzzsaw.subtitle: Actually better for sawing rocks.
quest.guide.items.buzzsaw.title: &eBuzzsaw
quest.guide.items.charger.desc_1: The &bInduction Charger&r allows the Accumulator Backpack to automatically recharge items that require FE.
quest.guide.items.charger.desc_2: &7&oPlace the Accumulator Backpack on the &bEngineer's Workbench &7to add or modify its attachments.&r
quest.guide.items.charger.title: Induction Charger
quest.guide.items.cjetpack.title: Copper Jetpack
quest.guide.items.cookinguide.desc_1: The &bCooking Guide&r allows recipies from &eFarmer's Delight&r to be automated.
quest.guide.items.cookinguide.desc_2: When combined with a &bBlaze Burner&r and a &bCooking Pot&r or &bSkillet&r, &bMechanical Arms&r can be used to place and remove items from them.
quest.guide.items.cookinguide.desc_3: &o&7Ponder this block by mousing over it and holding &6[W] &7for a detailed guide.&r
quest.guide.items.cookinguide.subtitle: Fast Food
quest.guide.items.cookinguide.title: Cooking Guide
quest.guide.items.extendogrip.desc_1: The Extendo Grip provides players with &e+4&r range.
quest.guide.items.extendogrip.desc_2: If used without a Backtank it will &ctake durability damage&r.
quest.guide.items.extendogrip.desc_3: A full Backtank will provide &e1000&r free uses of Extendo Grip.
quest.guide.items.extendogrip.desc_4: &o&7You can view extra information about this item by hovering over it and pressing &6[Shift]&7.&r
quest.guide.items.extendogrip.title: Extendo Grip
quest.guide.items.fillingtank.desc_1: Filling tanks will automatically transfer water to any items that require water in your inventory.
quest.guide.items.fillingtank.desc_2: Filling tanks can be filled by &6right-clicking&r on &bwater&r, or by using a &bspout&r, with each block providing &b100&r units of liquid.
quest.guide.items.fillingtank.desc_3: &o&7You can view extra information about this item by hovering over it and pressing &6[Shift]&7.&r
quest.guide.items.fillingtank.title: Small Filling Tank
quest.guide.items.floodlight.desc_1: Provides light and &bprevents mob spawns&r in a &632&r block cone.
quest.guide.items.floodlight.desc_2: The angle and rotation of the floodlight can be adjusted by &6[Right-Clicking]&r or &6[Shift-Right-Clicking]&r using an &bEngineer's Hammer&r.
quest.guide.items.floodlight.desc_3: Must be connected to an energy source via wires.
quest.guide.items.floodlight.desc_4: Requires a &credstone signal&r to light up. Invert this behaviour using an &bEngineer's Screwdriver&r.
quest.guide.items.floodlight.title: Flood Light
quest.guide.items.fuelingtank.desc_1: Fueling tanks will automatically transfer fuel to any items that require fuel in your inventory.
quest.guide.items.fuelingtank.desc_2: Fueling tanks can be filled by &6right-clicking&r on &blava&r, or by using a &bspout&r, with each block providing &b100&r units of fuel.
quest.guide.items.fuelingtank.desc_3: &o&7You can view extra information about this item by hovering over it and pressing &6[Shift]&7.&r
quest.guide.items.fuelingtank.title: Small Fueling Tank
quest.guide.items.heatsink.desc_1: The &bAdvanced Heatsinks&r allow the &bRailgun&r to charge much faster, allowing a higher fire rate.
quest.guide.items.heatsink.subtitle: Thank you, I'll have another
quest.guide.items.heatsink.title: Faster penetration
quest.guide.items.hook.desc_1: The grappling hook is a useful item that allows easier traversal around the environment during the early game.
quest.guide.items.hook.desc_2: Throw it with &6right-click&r, wait for it to attach, then &6right-click&r again to pull yourself towards the attach point.
quest.guide.items.hook.desc_3: Obtain &bsilk&r by breaking &bcocoons&r that spawn around the world.
quest.guide.items.hook.subtitle: Swing Swing
quest.guide.items.hook.title: Grappling Hook
quest.guide.items.hypertubes.desc_1: &bHypertubes&r can be used to set up quick transportation from point to point, given enough &eRotational Force&r to power them.
quest.guide.items.hypertubes.desc_2: This can be useful for travelling inside a base, or to nearby locations.
quest.guide.items.hypertubes.subtitle: Travelling with Style
quest.guide.items.hypertubes.title: Hypertubes
quest.guide.items.intro.desc_1: This chapter focuses on miscellaneous useful items that don't fit in any particular chapter.
quest.guide.items.intro.subtitle: I didn't know these existed...
quest.guide.items.intro.title: Useful Items
quest.guide.items.ironhead.title: Iron Drill Head
quest.guide.items.jerrycan.desc_1: &lThe Jerrycan can store up to &610&r buckets of Fuel &e(Gasoline, Diesel, Biodiesel)&r inside it, allowing the player to refuel their tools on the go.
quest.guide.items.jerrycan.desc_2: &6Craft&r the &bJerrycan&r with a &bMining Drill&r or &bBuzzsaw&r to refuel it.
quest.guide.items.jerrycan.desc_3: &cNote:&rThe Jerrycan cannot pick up liquids straight from the ground!
quest.guide.items.jerrycan.desc_4: &6Gas Stations&r are a good early-game location to find fuel.
quest.guide.items.jerrycan.subtitle: Feed The Machine
quest.guide.items.jerrycan.title: Jerrycan
quest.guide.items.lantern.desc_1: Provides light and &bprevents mob spawns&r in a &632&r block radious.
quest.guide.items.lantern.desc_2: Must be connected to an energy source via wires.
quest.guide.items.lantern.desc_3: Requires a &credstone signal&r to light up. Invert this behaviour using an &bEngineer's Screwdriver&r.
quest.guide.items.lantern.title: Powered Lantern
quest.guide.items.largetank.desc_1: Increases the fuel tank size of the mining drill or buzzsaw.
quest.guide.items.largetank.title: Large Tank
quest.guide.items.lubricate.desc_1: Makes the drill or sawblade take less damage per use.
quest.guide.items.lubricate.title: Advanced Lubrication System
quest.guide.items.miningdrill.desc_1: The mining drill allows players to break multiple blocks in one go, digging out a 3x3 area.
quest.guide.items.miningdrill.desc_3: &cNote:&r The Mining Drill requires Drill Heads and Fuel &e(Gasoline, Diesel, Biodiesel)&r to function.
quest.guide.items.miningdrill.desc_4: &7&oPlace the Mining Drill on the &bEngineer's Workbench &7to add a drill head and modify its attachments.&r
quest.guide.items.miningdrill.subtitle: The only tool you'll ever need.
quest.guide.items.miningdrill.title: &eMining Drill
quest.guide.items.nbacktank.title: Netherite Backtank
quest.guide.items.pipewrenches.desc_1: Not all wrenches can be used to configure pipes.
quest.guide.items.pipewrenches.desc_2: The supported wrenches are listed above.
quest.guide.items.pipewrenches.title: Supported Wrenches
quest.guide.items.pipez.desc_1: Pipes allow for faster and easier &btransportation&r of items, fluids or energy.
quest.guide.items.pipez.desc_2: By default, a pipe will only &cdeposit&r its contents into a connected block.
quest.guide.items.pipez.desc_3: To &bextract&r items, the pipe must be configured using &6[Shift-Right-Click]&r on the end connected to the block with an appropriate &bwrench&r.
quest.guide.items.pipez.desc_4: Pipes in extraction mode can be further configured by &6[Right-Clicking]&r it.
quest.guide.items.pipez.subtitle: Why would you ever use anything else?
quest.guide.items.pipez.title: Pipez
quest.guide.items.railgun.desc_1: The Railgun is a single target high damage ranged weapon, with &b100% armor penetration&r, making it suitable for use against bosses.
quest.guide.items.railgun.desc_2: Hold &6right-click&r to charge the Railgun, then release to fire once the gauge hits &b99%&r.
quest.guide.items.railgun.desc_3: The damage of the Railgun depends on the rod that it shoots.
quest.guide.items.railgun.desc_8: &7&oPlace the Railgun on the &bEngineer's Workbench &7to add or modify its attachments.&r
quest.guide.items.railgun.subtitle: That's a lotta damage
quest.guide.items.railgun.title: &eRailgun
quest.guide.items.richsoil.desc_1: Rich Soil allows crops and trees to grow &bsignificantly faster&r than planting them on regular dirt.
quest.guide.items.richsoil.desc_2: Rich Soil can be also be used in &bGarden Cloches&r, increasing the yield of crops.
quest.guide.items.richsoil.desc_3: &o&7For a list of crops boosted by Rich Soil, &6right click&7 on the entry for Rich Soil in &6JEI.
quest.guide.items.richsoil.subtitle: GROW!
quest.guide.items.richsoil.title: Rich Soil
quest.guide.items.rockcutter.desc_1: Use with the buzzsaw to grant it &bSilk Touch&r, as well as allow it to break blocks, at the cost of being bad at chopping trees.
quest.guide.items.rockcutter.title: Rockcutter Blade
quest.guide.items.sawblade.desc_1: Use with the Buzzsaw to allow it to function as a saw.
quest.guide.items.sawblade.desc_2: &8...Why would you make this? The drill is better...&r
quest.guide.items.sawblade.title: Sawblade
quest.guide.items.steelhead.title: Steel Drill Head
quest.guide.items.title: Useful Tools
quest.guide.items.wand.desc_1: The Wand of Symmetry allows the player to place blocks symmetrically.
quest.guide.items.wand.desc_2: &6[Right-Click]&r to place down a mirror axis.
quest.guide.items.wand.desc_3: &6[Shift Right-Click]&r to configure the wand's settings.
quest.guide.items.wand.desc_4: &o&7You can view extra information about this item by hovering over it and pressing &6[Shift]&7.&r
quest.guide.items.wand.subtitle: Mirror, Mirror
quest.guide.items.wand.title: Wand of Symmetry
quest.guide.oil.biomes.desc_1: Oil occurs more frequently in &l&eT3&r and &l&eT4&r biomes.
quest.guide.oil.biomes.desc_3: &o&7Use a &bNature's Compass&r &o&7to to search for the following biomes:&r
quest.guide.oil.biomes.subtitle: Time for some Freedom
quest.guide.oil.biomes.title: Oil Biomes
quest.guide.oil.products.desc_1: Pump &bCrude Oil&r into the &cbottom&r Fluid Tank of the &bDistiller&r, and supply a source of heat underneath it using a &eBurner&r or &eBlaze Burner&r.
quest.guide.oil.products.desc_2: If done correctly, the middle tank will start filling with &bDiesel&r, and the top tank will fill with &bGasoline&r.
quest.guide.oil.products.desc_3: &cNote:&r &7If either one of the output products fill their tanks, the &bDistiller&r &7will stop working!
quest.guide.oil.products.desc_5: &o&7A visual guide to setting up a distiller can be seen by Pondering &6[W]&r &o&7the Distillation Controller.&r
quest.guide.oil.products.title: Diesel and Gasoline
quest.guide.oil.scanner.desc_1: The &bOil Scanner&r allows players to check if a chunk contains oil.
quest.guide.oil.scanner.desc_2: Use it by &6[Right-Clicking]&r while having it equipped.
quest.guide.oil.scanner.desc_3: Once the scan completes, you will recieve 1 of 4 results, indicating the amount of oil present:
quest.guide.oil.scanner.desc_8: &cNote:&r Pressing &6[F3+G]&r can help to show where chunk borders are!
quest.guide.oil.scanner.desc_9: &o&7Make sure to Ponder&r &6[W]&r &o&7this item for usage instructions!&r
quest.guide.oil.scanner.subtitle: The Black Gold
quest.guide.oil.scanner.title: Finding Oil
quest.guide.oil.transportation.desc_1: It is likely that your oil setup will be located a significant distance away from your main base.
quest.guide.oil.transportation.desc_2: The most reliable way of transferring oil over long distances are:
quest.guide.oil.transportation.desc_3: &bRefined Storage&r &7> Network Transmitters and Network Recievers
quest.guide.oil.transportation.subtitle: Oil at Last
quest.guide.oil.transportation.title: Oil Transportation
quest.guide.parkour.desc_1: Many of the important skills are listed in the images below. For a more comprehensive guide, make sure to read through the §bParcool§r guidebook!
quest.guide.parkour.desc_2: Learning to use parkour properly will give you an advantage when navigating in the city - consider building a parkour course to familiarize yourself.
quest.guide.parkour.desc_3: If you want to §cdisable§r certain Parcool actions, press §6[Alt+P]§r to bring up the config menu.
quest.guide.parkour.subtitle: This book... it's written in parkour...
quest.guide.parkour.title: ParCool!
quest.guide.parkour.ziplinehook.desc_1: Parcool introduces §bziplines§r, which allow you to travel between buildings that are connected by zipline hooks.
quest.guide.parkour.ziplinehook.desc_2: To begin, craft a wooden or iron zipline hook.
quest.guide.parkour.ziplinehook.subtitle: Hang in there...
quest.guide.parkour.ziplinehook.title: Ziplines
quest.guide.parkour.ziplinerope.desc_1: Once your hooks are installed, you can connect them with zipline rope. §6[Shift-Right Click]§r to adjust the tension on the rope.
quest.guide.parkour.ziplinerope.desc_2: You can grab onto zipline ropes by jumping and holding right click.
quest.guide.parkour.ziplinerope.subtitle: Bridging the Gap
quest.guide.parkour.ziplinerope.title: Zipline Rope
quest.guide.radiation.155FCCF4F3DAF0EB.d: The Geiger Counter shows the &bambient radiation&r the player is exposed to.
It is recommended to equip one in your &6curio&r slot if you're doing anything involving radiation!
The &chigher&r the ambient radiation, the &cfaster&r your accumulated dose increases.
quest.guide.radiation.197C731CABE39E50.d: The &bGas Mask&r is the lowest tier of radiation-resistant armor. It is used in crafting the &bHazmat Helmet&r, which can then be upgraded into the &bBiohazard Helmet&r.
&cNOTE:&r The Gas Mask provides negligible head protection. It is recommended to upgrade to a better alternative as soon as possible!
quest.guide.radiation.197C731CABE39E50.s: Bulky, but better than chugging pills
quest.guide.radiation.1C2F4CB1AD817473.d: &bResistance Pills&r grant a temporary &65%&r reduction to all external sources of radiation.
They can be taken &64&r times, for a maximum reduction of &620%&r.
&7Note: These pills do not affect your &eaccumulated dose&7!&r
quest.guide.radiation.24AD58125F8E35B2.d: &bRadiation Pills&r reduce your &eaccumulated dose&r by &6250 mSv&r when taken.
&7Note: These pills do not affect the &eambient radiation&7 — seek shelter or remove the radiation source as soon as possible!&r
quest.guide.radiation.2AE5089E15D3C99A.d: The Dosimeter shows the player's &baccumulated radiation&r.
Excessively high accumulated radiation has the following effects:
Accumulated radiation can be reduced passively over time, or by consuming &bPills&r.
quest.guide.radiation.2D53348AD058C260.d: Most armors that provide radiation protection &cdegrade over time&r, and must be repaired using &bFilters&r.
quest.guide.radiation.6D5E969AAED05A9C.d: Crafting the Gas Mask requires a &bblueprint&r, which is sold by &eCharlie&r in the &6Retail District&r.
&cNOTE:&r &7You will have to complete his quests before you can trade with him.&r
quest.guide.radiation.desc_1: &bRadiation&r is emitted by certain types of blocks, as well as passively in the Wasteland. This effect occurs if the blocks are nearby or &cin your inventory&r, so be careful!
quest.guide.radiation.desc_2: Staying near sources of radiation for too long increases your &eaccumulated dose&r, which leads to &cnausea&r and eventually &cdeath&r.
quest.guide.radiation.desc_3: &7Blocks which emit radiation can be identified by the text &e[Emissions When Carried] &7in their description.&r
quest.guide.radiation.subtitle: Almost makes you wish for a nuclear winter
quest.guide.radiation.title: Radiation
quest.guide.scrapping.desc_1: Items with the §a🛠Scrappable§r tooltip can be recycled at a §bscrapping table§r.
quest.guide.scrapping.desc_2: Scrapped items will return §630%–70%§r of the items used to craft them.
quest.guide.scrapping.hammer.desc_1: To scrap an item, place a scrappable item on the table with &6[F]&r, then &6left click&r your &bscrapping hammer&r while looking at the item.
quest.guide.scrapping.hammer.desc_2: &cNOTE:&r Make sure to only left-click when the scrapping hammer is &bfully charged&r! The sword icon below your crosshair should fill up, then disappear.
quest.guide.scrapping.hammer.title: Scrapping Hammer
quest.guide.scrapping.subtitle: You've got the scraps... now to find a cave.
quest.guide.scrapping.title: Scrapping
quest.guide.storage.cable.desc_1: &bCables&r allow Refined Storage components to connect to each other without being directly adjacent.
quest.guide.storage.cable.desc_2: &7&oWhile you can obtain this item via crafting, consider looting it from &eT2 &7districts instead!
quest.guide.storage.cable.title: Cable
quest.guide.storage.cmanager.desc_1: Allows the user to view all patterns that are currently inside the crafters.
quest.guide.storage.cmanager.title: Crafter Manager
quest.guide.storage.cmonitor.desc_1: Allows players to watch the current ongoing crafting actions inside the network.
quest.guide.storage.cmonitor.title: Crafting Monitor
quest.guide.storage.crafter.desc_1: &bCrafters&r allow players to request &eautomated crafting&r of a recipe.
quest.guide.storage.crafter.desc_2: Crafters with an &bExact&r type Pattern can be placed anywhere, as long as they are connected to the network.
quest.guide.storage.crafter.desc_3: Crafters with a &bProcessing &rtype Pattern must be placed with the &edot on top facing the machine&r the item being processed needs to be sent into.
quest.guide.storage.crafter.subtitle: I DEMAND IT
quest.guide.storage.crafter.title: Crafter
quest.guide.storage.destructor.desc_1: &bDestructors&r will automatically &ebreak blocks&r placed in front of it.
quest.guide.storage.destructor.desc_2: The destructed blocks can be controlled by placing the desired block in its filter slots.
quest.guide.storage.destructor.desc_3: They can be also be toggled to drop the block &eas an item&r after breaking it instead of automatically importing it.
quest.guide.storage.destructor.subtitle: Removing from the World
quest.guide.storage.destructor.title: Destructor
quest.guide.storage.detector.desc_1: &bDetectors&r emit a redstone signal depending on the amount of a desired item, block or fluid present inside the network.
quest.guide.storage.detector.desc_2: This amount can be configured by &6[Right-Clicking]&r on the detector.
quest.guide.storage.detector.title: Detector
quest.guide.storage.external.desc_1: The &bExternal Storage&r allows the network to &eaccess the contents&r of containers placed in the world, essentially making them act like storage disks.
quest.guide.storage.external.desc_2: This can be combined with &bStorage Drawers&r to allow easy storage of massive amounts of items.
quest.guide.storage.external.title: External Storage
quest.guide.storage.fgrid.desc_1: The &bFluid Grid&r allows storing and retrieving of &efluids&r from the network.
quest.guide.storage.fgrid.desc_2: &cNote&r: It is advised to store fluids using tanks from other mods connected by an &bExternal Storage&r, since the disks required to store fluids are not lootble and complex to craft for newer players.
quest.guide.storage.fgrid.desc_3: &7&oWhile you can obtain this item via crafting, consider looting it from &eT2 &7districts instead!
quest.guide.storage.fgrid.title: Fluid Grid
quest.guide.storage.interface.desc_1: &bInterfaces&r allow the logistic systems of other mods &e(Pipez, Create, etc)&r to interact with items in the Refined Storage network.
quest.guide.storage.interface.desc_2: They have their own inventory which can store a set amount of items, which can be configured by &6[Right-Clicking]&r on it.
quest.guide.storage.interface.title: Interface
quest.guide.storage.interfacef.desc_1: &bFluid Interfaces&r act exactly like the normal Interface, but for liquids instead.
quest.guide.storage.interfacef.title: Fluid Interface
quest.guide.storage.manipulator.desc_1: The &bDisk Manipulator&r allows transfer of contents between disks and the storage system.
quest.guide.storage.manipulator.desc_2: What can be transfered into or from a disk can be configured.
quest.guide.storage.manipulator.subtitle: I think we should reconsider our relationship
quest.guide.storage.manipulator.title: Disk Manipulator
quest.guide.storage.nreciver.desc_1: The &bNetwork Reciever&r allows for recieving of a network's data, if paired with a &bNetwork Transmitter&r using a &bNetwork Card&r.
quest.guide.storage.nreciver.desc_2: &cNOTE:&r Both the Reciever and Transmitter must be &echunkloaded&r in order to function.
quest.guide.storage.nreciver.title: Network Receiver
quest.guide.storage.ntransmitter.desc_1: The &bNetwork Transmitter&r allows the main network to be accessible from anywhere in the world, if paired with a &bNetwork Reciever&r using a &bNetwork Card&r.
quest.guide.storage.ntransmitter.desc_2: &cNOTE:&r Both the Reciever and Transmitter must be &echunkloaded&r in order to function.
quest.guide.storage.ntransmitter.title: Network Transmitter
quest.guide.storage.portable.desc_1: The &bPortable Grid&r acts as a miniature Refined Storage Network, allowing you to carry one disk's worth of items on the go.
quest.guide.storage.portable.desc_2: It needs to be charged with &6FE&r, and uses energy every time an item is &einserted&r or &eremoved&r from it.
quest.guide.storage.portable.desc_3: Charge it by connecting it to a power source, or use a &bBackpack&r with a &bBattery Upgrade&r.
quest.guide.storage.portable.desc_4: &7&oWhile you can obtain this item via crafting, consider looting it from &eT2 &7districts instead!
quest.guide.storage.portable.subtitle: View my items
quest.guide.storage.portable.title: Portable Grid
quest.guide.storage.relay.desc_1: &bRelays&r act as a cable that can be toggled on and off, allowing players to split the network.
quest.guide.storage.relay.title: Relay
quest.guide.storage.smanager.desc_1: The &bSecurity Manager&r allows the owner of the network &e(the player who places the Security Manager)&r to assign permissions or restrictions for other players.
quest.guide.storage.smanager.desc_2: Permissions can be set for a player by placing a bound &bSecurity Card&r in the &ebottom slot&r, and then storing the card in the &etop slots&r.
quest.guide.storage.smanager.desc_3: &cWARNING:&r This does &cnot&r prevent interacting or destroying Refined Storage blocks through other means!
quest.guide.storage.smanager.title: Security Manager
quest.guide.storage.speed.title: Speed Upgrade
quest.guide.storage.stack.desc_1: Increases the &bstack size&r of items transferred.
quest.guide.storage.stack.title: Stack Upgrade
quest.guide.storage.transmitter.desc_1: Wireless Transmitters allow &bWireless Grids&r to function when within range.
quest.guide.storage.transmitter.desc_2: The base range of a transmitter is &616&r blocks.
quest.guide.storage.transmitter.subtitle: Short Range Networks
quest.guide.storage.transmitter.title: Wireless Transmitter
quest.guide.storage.wgrids.desc_1: Wireless Grids can access the main network remotely when within range of a &bWireless Transmitter&r.
quest.guide.storage.wgrids.desc_2: They function the same as the &bGrid &rused to craft them.
quest.guide.storage.wgrids.desc_3: &o&7Using the Wireless grid requires energy, which can be recharged using the following:&r
quest.guide.storage.wgrids.title: Wireless Grids
quest.guide.survival.bandages.desc_1: While better than bandages, consider saving up to craft these into §bmedkits§r instead, which are much more powerful.
quest.guide.survival.bandages.subtitle: For larger wounds.
quest.guide.survival.bandages.title: I need healing!
quest.guide.survival.cooking_pot.desc_1: Boil a water bucket in a §bcooking pot§r to get 16 purified water, which you can take out with a glass bottle. Remember to retrieve your bucket!
quest.guide.survival.cooking_pot.title: Let Him Cook - II
quest.guide.survival.death.desc_1: Death will cause you to lose some of the §bbonus hearts§r gained from eating different types of food. You can regain these hearts by re-eating the forgotten foods.
quest.guide.survival.death.desc_2: The minimum amount of hearts you can have is §610§r. If you are still learning, it may be wise to wait till you are better prepared before trying to gain extra hearts.
quest.guide.survival.death.desc_3: §6Vanilla hearts and body part health are linked.§r Damage dealt to an already destroyed limb will instead be directed to another limb, so watch your health!
quest.guide.survival.death.desc_4: Natural regeneration by food has been §6drastically reduced§r. You will have to regain hearts using healing items, potions, or sleeping in a bed.
quest.guide.survival.death.subtitle: Ask not for whom the bell tolls...
quest.guide.survival.death.title: DEATH
quest.guide.survival.fiber.desc_1: Collect §bhemp seeds§r from grass and plant them to start growing hemp plants. It's recommended you keep a healthy amount of these, as crafting string uses a lot of fibre.
quest.guide.survival.fiber.subtitle: Hemp 101
quest.guide.survival.fiber.title: Fiber on Demand
quest.guide.survival.furnace.desc_1: Boil water in a §bfurnace§r to purify it, ensuring it is safe for consumption.
quest.guide.survival.furnace.subtitle: The normal way.
quest.guide.survival.furnace.title: Let Him Cook - I
quest.guide.survival.hard_falling.desc_1: Fall damage is §cincreased§r by 20%% per level.
quest.guide.survival.hard_falling.subtit: Caused by damage to your legs.
quest.guide.survival.hard_falling.title: Hard Falling
quest.guide.survival.headache.subtitle: Caused by damage to your head.
quest.guide.survival.headache.title: Headache
quest.guide.survival.hearts.desc_1: Crystal Hearts can be looted from §bhospital buildings§r. Each one increases your max health by §bone heart§r, up to a total of 10 hearts.
quest.guide.survival.hearts.subtitle: Me and my broken heart...
quest.guide.survival.hearts.title: Crystal Hearts
quest.guide.survival.herbs.desc_1: Healing herbs are essential for crafting higher tier recovery items. Unfortunately, they can only be looted from §bmedical buildings§r.
quest.guide.survival.herbs.subtitle: It's the green, it's the green, it's the green you need...
quest.guide.survival.herbs.title: Healing Herbs
quest.guide.survival.life.desc_1: Managing your health is essential to survival. This modpack has §6body part damage§r, which means limbs can be damaged, causing debuffs.
quest.guide.survival.life.desc_2: You can check the status of your limbs at any time by pressing §6[H]§r to bring up the health tab.
quest.guide.survival.life.desc_3: A list of debuffs caused by damage to each limb is provided below.
quest.guide.survival.life.subtitle: Welcome to the world.
quest.guide.survival.life.title: LIFE
quest.guide.survival.medkit.desc_1: The best tier of medical treatment. You could craft these... but perhaps it would be better to try and find them in §bhospitals§r.
quest.guide.survival.medkit.subtitle: ...I guess we're sewing your arm back on.
quest.guide.survival.medkit.title: I NEED HEALING!!
quest.guide.survival.mining_fatigue.subt: Caused by damage to your arms.
quest.guide.survival.plaster.desc_1: Plasters don't heal much, but they are cheap to craft.
quest.guide.survival.plaster.subtitle: For the small ouchies.
quest.guide.survival.plaster.title: I need healing.
quest.guide.survival.sink.desc_1: Some sinks can provide an endless supply of water, but keep in mind it still needs to be boiled!
quest.guide.survival.sink.subtitle: Let that Sink In
quest.guide.survival.sink.title: Water on Demand
quest.guide.survival.slowness.subtitle: Caused by damage to your feet.
quest.guide.survival.slowness.title: Slowness
quest.guide.survival.string.desc_1: Craft hemp into string, or loot it from cities.
quest.guide.survival.string.title: String
quest.guide.survival.subtitle: AKA: How to not die instantly.
quest.guide.survival.thirst.desc_1: Bottled water is §cdirty§r by default, and must be purified by boiling in order to safely be drunk.
quest.guide.survival.thirst.subtitle: Be like water.
quest.guide.survival.thirst.title: Dealing With Thirst
quest.guide.survival.title: Survival
quest.guide.survival.tonic.title: Tonic
quest.guide.survival.vulnerability.desc_: All damage taken from any source except falling is §cincreased§r by 20%% per level.
quest.guide.survival.vulnerability.subti: Caused by damage to your torso.
quest.guide.survival.vulnerability.title: Vulnerability
quest.guide.survival.water.desc_1: Once purified, water can be safely consumed.
quest.guide.survival.water.subtitle: Crystal clear.
quest.guide.survival.water.title: Purified Water
quest.guide.survival.weakness.desc_1: §cWeakens§r your attack damage, possibly reducing it to 0 if it's already low enough.
quest.guide.survival.weakness.subtitle: Caused by damage to your arms.
quest.guide.survival.weakness.title: Weakness
quest.guide.survival.wool.desc_1: Craft string into wool. Or start a sheep farm.
quest.guide.survival.wool.title: Wool
quest.guide.temperature.adapt_1.desc_1: Coat your armor to increase §dboth resistances§r by 3 points.
quest.guide.temperature.adapt_1.title: Adaptive Insulation - I
quest.guide.temperature.adapt_2.desc_1: Coat your armor to increase §dboth resistances§r by 5 points.
quest.guide.temperature.adapt_2.title: Adaptive Insulation - II
quest.guide.temperature.adapt_3.desc_1: Coat your armor to increase §dboth resistances§r by 7 points.
quest.guide.temperature.adapt_3.title: Adaptive Insulation - III
quest.guide.temperature.boiler.desc_1: Boilers provide heat in a radius if &6powered by redstone&r and provided with §bburnable materials§r like coal.
quest.guide.temperature.boiler.subtitle: Getting hot in here...
quest.guide.temperature.boiler.title: Warm Me Up
quest.guide.temperature.calendar.desc_1: Temperature is also affected by &6season&r, which can be determined using a &bcalendar&r.
quest.guide.temperature.calendar.desc_2: &6Summer&r and &6Winter&r are the coldest and hottest seasons respectively, so plan your scavenging trips well!
quest.guide.temperature.calendar.desc_3: You can place the calendar in an &bitem frame&r to be able to tell the current season at a glance.
quest.guide.temperature.calendar.title: Calendar
quest.guide.temperature.cold_1.desc_1: Coat your armor to increase §6heat resistance§r by 3 points.
quest.guide.temperature.cold_1.title: Beating the Heat - I
quest.guide.temperature.cold_2.desc_1: Coat your armor to increase §6heat resistance§r by 5 points.
quest.guide.temperature.cold_2.title: Beating the Heat - II
quest.guide.temperature.cold_3.desc_1: Coat your armor to increase §6heat resistance§r by 7 points.
quest.guide.temperature.cold_3.title: Beating the Heat - III
quest.guide.temperature.cooler.desc_1: Creating and powering a &bcooler&r with &6SU&r will allow it to cool an area, without the need for fuel.
quest.guide.temperature.cooler.subtitle: Your Very Own Fridge
quest.guide.temperature.cooler.title: Automated Cooling
quest.guide.temperature.heater.desc_1: Creating and powering a &bheater&r with &6SU&r will allow it to heat an area, without the need for fuel.
quest.guide.temperature.heater.subtitle: Cold Remover 9000
quest.guide.temperature.heater.title: Automated Heating
quest.guide.temperature.hot_1.desc_1: Coat your armor to increase §bcold resistance§r by 3 points.
quest.guide.temperature.hot_1.title: Bracing the Cold - I
quest.guide.temperature.hot_2.desc_1: Coat your armor to increase §bcold resistance§r by 5 points.
quest.guide.temperature.hot_2.title: Bracing the Cold - II
quest.guide.temperature.hot_3.desc_1: Coat your armor to increase §bcold resistance§r by 7 points.
quest.guide.temperature.hot_3.title: Bracing the Cold - III
quest.guide.temperature.icebox.desc_1: Iceboxes provide chill in an area if &6powered by redstone&r and provided with §bfrozen items§r like ice.
quest.guide.temperature.icebox.subtitle: Chill out, bro...
quest.guide.temperature.icebox.title: Cool Me Down
quest.guide.temperature.insulation.desc_: A grace period of &65 minutes&r is given on every respawn during which you are immune to changes in temperature.
However, venturing into higher tier &eDistricts&r require &binsulating your armor&r, and you will quickly freeze or overheat if unprepared.
Find or craft and use a §bsewing table§r to insulate your armor with various materials.
quest.guide.temperature.insulation.title: Insulating Armor
quest.guide.temperature.subtitle: Ahhhh, that's hot.
quest.guide.temperature.thermometer.desc: A thermometer can accurately measure the present temperature of the environment.
Craft or find one from &bresidential houses&r in the &eSuburb Residential District&r.
quest.guide.temperature.title: Temperature
quest.guide.tutorial.advanced_armor.desc: &bAdvanced Armor Components&r are required to make and repair &enetherite&r gear.
&o&7A list of &eTier 4 &7armor sets can be found in the &6Armor Sets &7chapter.
quest.guide.tutorial.base.desc_1: Consider locating a suitable place to establish a &bpermanent base&r. Popular choices are tall buildings, or easily defendable open fields.
quest.guide.tutorial.base.desc_2: &7You can find specific buildings to live in using the &bBuilding Radar&7§r.
quest.guide.tutorial.base.subtitle: A place to call Home
quest.guide.tutorial.base.title: Settling Down
quest.guide.tutorial.basic_armor.desc_1: §bBasic Armor Components§r are required to make and repair §eiron§r gear.
quest.guide.tutorial.basic_armor.desc_2: It is recommended to save armor components for repairs, as iron gear can be easily looted in the city.
quest.guide.tutorial.basic_armor.desc_3: &o&7A list of &eTier 2 &7armor sets can be found in the &6Armor Sets &7chapter.
quest.guide.tutorial.basic_armor.title: §aLoot§r - Basic Armor Components
quest.guide.tutorial.canteen.desc_1: §bCanteens§r can be looted from suburban houses, or crafted.
quest.guide.tutorial.canteen.desc_2: An added benefit is that water stored in it will be §bautomatically purified§r. However, looted drinks will usually be a more effective way of restoring thirst.
quest.guide.tutorial.canteen.desc_3: &o&7Information on how to obtain and purify water can be found in the &6Survival &7chapter.&r
quest.guide.tutorial.canteen.subtitle: Compact water source
quest.guide.tutorial.canteen.title: §9Craft§r or §aLoot§r - Canteen
quest.guide.tutorial.coldbiomes.desc_1: Insulate your armor with &bcommon warming coats&r before travelling to the &eHighrise Residential District&r.
quest.guide.tutorial.coldbiomes.desc_2: &cWARNING:&r It is recommended to insulate all &l&c4&r pieces of your armor — while you can travel with less, you may still freeze or overheat depending on time of day and season!
quest.guide.tutorial.coldbiomes.desc_3: &o&7Refer to the &6Temperature &7chapter for more information on temperature management and insulation.&r
quest.guide.tutorial.coldbiomes.title: Moving Onwards: &eCold Biomes
quest.guide.tutorial.colderbiomes.desc_1: Insulate your armor with &brare warming coats&r before travelling to the &eRetail District&r.
quest.guide.tutorial.colderbiomes.desc_2: &cWARNING:&r It is recommended to insulate all &l&c4&r pieces of your armor — while you can travel with less, you may still freeze or overheat depending on time of day and season!
quest.guide.tutorial.colderbiomes.desc_3: &o&7Refer to the &6Temperature &7chapter for more information on temperature management and insulation.&r
quest.guide.tutorial.colderbiomes.title: Moving Onwards: &eColder Biomes
quest.guide.tutorial.goldenapple.desc_1: The only cure for the infection is by eating §bgolden apples§r.
quest.guide.tutorial.goldenapple.desc_2: As they cannot be crafted yet, you will have to loot them from §bmedical buildings§r.
quest.guide.tutorial.goldenapple.desc_3: In your starting district, this is the §bPolyclinic§r — but beware, it is heavily guarded.
quest.guide.tutorial.goldenapple.subtitl: An apple a day keeps the zombies away.
quest.guide.tutorial.goldenapple.title: §aLoot§r - Golden Apples
quest.guide.tutorial.healing.desc_1: Looted §bstring§r and §bwool§r can be crafted into §bbandages§r, which provide a small amount of healing.
quest.guide.tutorial.healing.desc_2: You should aim to have a healthy supply of these, as they are the main way of restoring health.
quest.guide.tutorial.healing.desc_3: &o&7Information on the body part health system in this mod, as well as how to craft and use medical items can be found in the &6Survival &7chapter.&r
quest.guide.tutorial.healing.subtitle: Patch yourself up
quest.guide.tutorial.healing.title: §9Craft§r or §aLoot§r - Healing Items
quest.guide.tutorial.hotbiomes.desc_1: Insulate your armor with &bcommon cooling coats&r before travelling to the &eHighrise Office District&r.
quest.guide.tutorial.hotbiomes.desc_2: &cWARNING:&r It is recommended to insulate all &l&c4&r pieces of your armor — while you can travel with less, you may still freeze or overheat depending on time of day and season!
quest.guide.tutorial.hotbiomes.desc_3: &o&7Refer to the &6Temperature &7chapter for more information on temperature management and insulation.&r
quest.guide.tutorial.hotbiomes.title: Moving Onwards: &eHot Biomes
quest.guide.tutorial.hotelloot.desc_1: The &eHotel District&r is an excellent source of &bmoney&r, which can be used to trade with NPCs once you have completed their story quests.
quest.guide.tutorial.hotelloot.desc_2: &bIodine Pills&r and radiation gear can be found in the hospital.
quest.guide.tutorial.hotelloot.desc_3: It also is the best place to loot &brifles&r and their ammuntion.
quest.guide.tutorial.hotelloot.desc_4: &o&7A rough outline of the loot table for each building can be found in the &6Districts&r &7chapter, once you have discovered the building.&r
quest.guide.tutorial.hotelloot.title: District Loot
quest.guide.tutorial.hotterbiomes.desc_1: Insulate your armor with &brare cooling coats&r before travelling to the &eHighrise Hotel District&r.
quest.guide.tutorial.hotterbiomes.desc_2: &cWARNING:&r It is recommended to insulate all &l&c4&r pieces of your armor — while you can travel with less, you may still freeze or overheat depending on time of day and season!
quest.guide.tutorial.hotterbiomes.desc_3: &o&7Refer to the &6Temperature &7chapter for more information on temperature management and insulation.&r
quest.guide.tutorial.hotterbiomes.title: Moving Onwards: &eHotter Biomes
quest.guide.tutorial.infection.desc_1: All zombies have a chance to §cinfect§r their victims upon making contact during an attack.
quest.guide.tutorial.infection.desc_2: The infection progresses slowly through &6&l5&r&r stages. Letting it take over will result in your character becoming a zombie.
quest.guide.tutorial.infection.desc_3: Certain armor types can negate this effect.
quest.guide.tutorial.infection.desc_4: Different variation of zombies have different infection chance.
quest.guide.tutorial.infection.subtitle: One bite is all it takes… well, maybe a couple.
quest.guide.tutorial.infection.title: Unavoidable Infection
§bIntermediate Armor Components§r are required to make and repair §esteel§r gear.
Steel gear is quite §crare§r, and it is likely you will need to §bmanually craft§r a set.
&o&7A list of &eTier 3 &7armor sets can be found in the &6Armor Sets &7chapter.
quest.guide.tutorial.iron.desc_1: §eTier 2§r districts contain iron, which can be used to craft the second tier of weapons and armor.
quest.guide.tutorial.iron.desc_2: &o&7Refer to the &6Armor Sets &7and &6Melee Weapons &7chapters for a list of all craftable weapons and armor.&r
quest.guide.tutorial.iron.subtitle: The Iron Age
quest.guide.tutorial.iron.title: §aLoot§r - Iron
quest.guide.tutorial.netherite.desc_1: The &eWasteland&r is the primary source of &bAncient Debris&r.
quest.guide.tutorial.netherite.desc_2: Processing it into &bNetherite&r will require the use of an &bArc Furnace&r.
quest.guide.tutorial.netherite.desc_3: &7&oSteps to producing steel can be found in the &6Immersive Engineering &7chapter.&r
quest.guide.tutorial.netherite.subtitle: The Netherite Age
quest.guide.tutorial.netherite.title: §aLoot§r - Netherite
quest.guide.tutorial.npcs.desc_1: NPCs can be picked up and moved around by &6sneak-right clicking them with an empty hand&r.
quest.guide.tutorial.npcs.desc_2: For best results, aim down at their feet.
quest.guide.tutorial.npcs.desc_3: You can then place them in a &btrain&r, or carry them while &bdriving&r to move them to safer locations.
quest.guide.tutorial.npcs.desc_4: &7For more information on where NPCs can be found, consult the respective &6District Chapter&r.
quest.guide.tutorial.npcs.subtitle: You can take them home
quest.guide.tutorial.npcs.title: Moving Survivors
quest.guide.tutorial.officeloot.desc_1: The &eHighrise Office District&r contains an abundance of &bRefined Storage&r components, as well as general redstone related items.
quest.guide.tutorial.officeloot.desc_2: It also is a decent source of &bblaze rods&r and brewing ingredients.
quest.guide.tutorial.officeloot.desc_3: &o&7A rough outline of the loot table for each building can be found in the &6Districts&r &7chapter, once you have discovered the building.&r
quest.guide.tutorial.officeloot.title: District Loot
quest.guide.tutorial.power.desc_1: Many components needed to set up both §6Create§r and §6Immersive Engineering§r's power generation can be looted in the city.
quest.guide.tutorial.power.desc_2: For now, the simplest power sources would be §bWater Wheels§r for §6Create§r, and §bThermoelectric Generators§r for §6Immersive Engineering§r.
quest.guide.tutorial.power.subtitle: Keeping the lights on
quest.guide.tutorial.power.title: §6Tech§r | §aLoot§r - Power
quest.guide.tutorial.radiation.desc_1: Travelling to the &eWasteland&r or dealing with radioactive blocks such as &bUranium&r will slowly irradiate you, which will cause &cnegative effects&r and eventually, &cdeath&r.
quest.guide.tutorial.radiation.desc_2: &cWARNING:&r It is highly recommended to familarize yourself with the &6Radiation&r chapter of the guidebook before you proceed further.
quest.guide.tutorial.radiation.subtitle: Everything's green from here on out.
quest.guide.tutorial.radiation.title: Dealing with Radiation
quest.guide.tutorial.residentialloot.des: The &eHighrise Residential District&r contains backpack upgrades, protection upgrade parts, and &bT2 resources&r.
It also is a decent source of &barmor components&r, ammo, and weapons.
&o&7A rough outline of the loot table for each building can be found in the &6Districts&r &7chapter, once you have discovered the building.&r
quest.guide.tutorial.retailloot.desc_1: The &eRetail District&r contains the &bGunstore&r, as well as &biodine pills&r and radiation gear in the Terrace Plaza Mall.
quest.guide.tutorial.retailloot.desc_2: Shops containing loot for &bImmersive Engineering&r and &bCreate&r components can also be found here.
quest.guide.tutorial.retailloot.desc_3: &o&7A rough outline of the loot table for each building can be found in the &6Districts&r &7chapter, once you have discovered the building.&r
quest.guide.tutorial.retailloot.title: District Loot
quest.guide.tutorial.seek_cure.desc_1: You may have learned to survive, but rebuilding civilization will take more than just skill and perseverance.
quest.guide.tutorial.seek_cure.desc_2: To truly escape the infection, you must learn to conquer it, a process which starts by rediscovering the methods used to craft the cure.
quest.guide.tutorial.seek_cure.subtitle: A New Chapter Begins
quest.guide.tutorial.seek_cure.title: Rediscovering the Cure
quest.guide.tutorial.steel.desc_1: If you did not automate it, §bsteel§r can be looted in §eTier 3§r districts.
quest.guide.tutorial.steel.desc_3: &7&oSteps to producing steel can be found in the &6Immersive Engineering &7and &6Advanced Materials &7chapters.&r
quest.guide.tutorial.steel.subtitle: The Steel Age
quest.guide.tutorial.steel.title: §aLoot§r - Steel
quest.guide.tutorial.steel_early.desc_1: §6Immersive Engineering§r allows for the production of §bsteel§r from §biron§r, a §eTier 3§r material.
quest.guide.tutorial.steel_early.desc_2: If you have an established base, setting up §bsteel production§r will make exploring further much easier.
quest.guide.tutorial.steel_early.title: §6Tech§r - Early Steel
quest.guide.tutorial.storage.desc_1: §bOffice buildings§r in §eTier 2§r districts contain an abundance of §6Refined Storage§r loot.
quest.guide.tutorial.storage.desc_2: This allows you to easily set up a §bRefined Storage system§r, for easier management of all your items.
quest.guide.tutorial.storage.desc_3: A basic guide to Refined Storage can be found in the §6Refined Storage§r chapter.
quest.guide.tutorial.storage.subtitle: It's not a hoarding problem, I swear
quest.guide.tutorial.storage.title: §6Tech§r | §aLoot§r - Refined Storage
quest.guide.tutorial.supplies.desc_1: Weapons, armor, and food can be looted from &bsuburban houses&r.
quest.guide.tutorial.supplies.subtitle: Can't fight on an empty stomach
quest.guide.tutorial.supplies.title: §aLoot§r - Supplies and Weapons
quest.guide.tutorial.tier_1_districts.de: This modpack is split into &l&66&r districts, each with increasing difficulty and more extreme temperatures.
Your starting district is the &eSuburb Residential&r district, which is relatively peaceful.
&cWe recommend gearing up before trying to tackle the other districts.&r
&7Each district spawns in a limited set of biomes, a list of which can be found in the &6Districts &7chapter.&r
quest.guide.tutorial.tier_1_districts.su: What do you mean people live in these?
quest.guide.tutorial.vehicles.desc_1: It is likely some districts will be located §ca considerable distance away§r from your starting location.
quest.guide.tutorial.vehicles.desc_2: Consider making or finding a §bcar§r, §btrain§r or §bplane§r to shorten the travel time!
quest.guide.tutorial.vehicles.desc_3: Chapters on how to make vehicles can be found in the §6Transportation§r tab.
quest.guide.tutorial.vehicles.subtitle: Are we there yet
quest.guide.tutorial.vehicles.title: §6Tech§r | §9Craft§r - Vehicles
quest.guide.tutorial.wastelandloot.desc_: The &eWasteland District&r contains the &bC-07 Laboratory&r, as well as the &bMilitary Base&r.
It is rich in &bNetherite&r and advanced materials. High-tier &bweapons&r and &bammunition&r can also be found.
&o&7A rough outline of the loot table for each building can be found in the &6Districts&r &7chapter, once you have discovered the building.&r
quest.guide.tutorial.wastelandloot.title: District Loot
quest.guide.world.animal_pen.desc_1: You can craft §banimal pens§r to compact large herds of animals into a single block.
quest.guide.world.animal_pen.desc_2: Not only is this better for performance, it also allows for easier interaction and harvesting.
quest.guide.world.animal_pen.subtitle: How do they all fit in there?
quest.guide.world.animal_pen.title: §9Craft§r - Animal Pens
quest.guide.world.backpack.desc_1: &bBackpacks&r can be looted from suburban houses, or crafted.
quest.guide.world.backpack.desc_2: §cNOTE:§r You are limited to only having §bone§r backpack at a time. Carrying more backpacks in your inventory will §cslow you down§r!
quest.guide.world.backpack.subtitle: Everything including the kitchen sink.
quest.guide.world.backpack.title: &9Craft&r or &aLoot&r - Backpack
quest.guide.world.backpackupgrade.desc_1: Backpacks can be further &bupgraded&r to expand their inventory capacity, or to add useful features.
quest.guide.world.backpackupgrade.desc_2: A selection of useful upgrades are listed, but there are many more. Experiment to see what works for you!
quest.guide.world.backpackupgrade.desc_3: All backpack upgrades use an &bUpgrade Base&r.
quest.guide.world.backpackupgrade.title: Backpack Upgrades
quest.guide.world.compass.desc_1: Each district spawns in a certain set of biomes, which can be located using the &bNature's Compass.&r
quest.guide.world.compass.desc_2: &7Each district spawns in a limited set of biomes, a list of which can be found in the &6Districts &7chapter.&r
quest.guide.world.compass.desc_3: The Nature's Compass needs to be &bcharged&r, which can be done by crafting it with a &bBattery&r. Locating a biome costs &aEXP&r.
quest.guide.world.compass.desc_4: §cNOTE:§r The compass will always guide you to the closest biome of that type within §610000§r blocks, so travel in the opposite direction if the biome you find doesn't end up suitable for you!
quest.guide.world.compass.subtitle: Guide to Nowhere
quest.guide.world.compass.title: §9Craft§r - Nature's Compass
quest.guide.world.foodhealth.desc_1: The more types of food you consume, the more §bbonus hearts§r you get.
quest.guide.world.foodhealth.desc_2: This guidebook serves as a tracker for the foods you have eaten.
quest.guide.world.foodhealth.desc_3: §cWARNING:§r You will lose bonus hearts on death during hard mode, causing you to have to re-eat foods you have already eaten!
quest.guide.world.foodhealth.subtitle: Stress eating.
quest.guide.world.foodhealth.title: More Food, More Health
quest.guide.world.kitchen.desc_1: If you have the resources, consider preparing your food instead of eating it raw, as cooked meals restore more hunger.
quest.guide.world.kitchen.desc_2: You will need a &bPot&r on top of a &bStove&r in order to cook meals. Both of these can be looted from houses.
quest.guide.world.kitchen.subtitle: Mama will help you
quest.guide.world.kitchen.title: Kitchen 101
quest.guide.world.nether.desc_1: The Nether and the End do §cnot§r exist in this modpack. Instead, you will have to loot their resources from the city.
quest.guide.world.nether.subtitle: Just a figment of your imagination... or maybe?
quest.guide.world.nether.title: The Nether and the End
quest.guide.world.specialization.desc_1: Pressing §6[F10]§r will open up your skill tree. Once enough EXP is earned in a skill, it will provide a perk point that can be used to enhance your abilities.
quest.guide.world.specialization.desc_2: Skills can be levelled up by doing their respective actions:
quest.guide.world.specialization.title: Talents and Specialization
quest.guide.world.subway.desc_1: &bSubway tunnels&r are ideal for travelling long distances between cities.
quest.guide.world.subway.desc_2: While it would be best to bring a car or build a train, simply running through them can be a safe way to navigate the world.
quest.guide.world.subway.desc_3: Locate a &bsubway station&r, or dig down to the tunnels.
quest.guide.world.subway.subtitle: The Routes Beneath
quest.guide.world.subway.title: The Subway
